mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for the S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, after a trigger event, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is high and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also elevated, it signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the Fed, which increases borrowing costs and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL meet the trigger conditions, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are positive on average over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a significant slowdown in the construction sector and broader economic demand, which can precede a general economic downturn and negatively affect equity markets.
failure_condition: If a 10% MoM decline in HOUST is followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences over a 7-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing and mining activity, signaling a healthy supply side of the economy that supports corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with INDPRO growth over any 3-year rolling window during periods of economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a weakening labor market, signaling reduced consumer income and spending, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If, following a trigger event, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 15-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above a certain threshold indicates restrictive monetary policy, which increases the cost of capital for businesses and dampens economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are positive on average during periods when FEDFUNDS consistently remains above 5.0% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates broad-based economic strength and consumer confidence, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 3.0% YoY AND RSAFS growth > 5.0% YoY
failure_condition: If, when both GDPC1 and RSAFS meet the trigger conditions, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative on average over a 10-year period.
